title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via Release Profiles

cvss_v3_1.notes: PR:L because API key required. A:H because the regex runs during RSS sync, hanging the application indefinitely.

description:
The PerlRegexFactory.CreateRegex() method creates .NET Regex objects from user-supplied patterns without setting a MatchTimeout. Patterns are stored in release profiles via the /api/v3/releaseprofile API endpoint. When RSS sync evaluates releases against these profiles, the TermMatcherService passes each release title through the regex. A catastrophic backtracking pattern like /(a+)+$/ causes exponential processing time on non-matching input, hanging the Radarr process.

The compiled regex is cached for 24 hours (TermMatcherService.GetMatcher), so a single malicious profile persists through the cache lifetime. The DoS is triggered automatically on every RSS sync cycle.

root_cause.detail: No MatchTimeout parameter. Should use: new Regex(pattern, options | RegexOptions.Compiled, TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1))

remediation: Add MatchTimeout to all Regex constructors that accept user input: new Regex(pattern, options, TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1))
Validate regex patterns for known catastrophic backtracking indicators before compilation
Consider using a regex complexity analyzer or limiting pattern length
